title: 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seven MassDOT employees are on leave following a Boston News 25 investigation into alleged timesheet falsifications for overtime.
The investigation tracked employee vehicles, revealing discrepancies between work hours recorded and actual departure times from the facility.
Reported instances include an employee earning $149,000 in overtime despite leaving work early on multiple occasions.

title: Theo Epstein shares his thoughts on state of 2026 Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Theo Epstein, former Red Sox GM, believes the team can overcome its current 22-27 record through improved offense.
The Red Sox have struggled offensively but maintained strong pitching and defense, according to Epstein.
Epstein stresses trust and honesty as crucial for building a sustainable, contending MLB team.

title: Person who attacked Harvard student from behind snuck into MIT dorm shortly after, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A person matching the suspect's description sneaked into Harvard's Lowell House and attacked a student Tuesday night.
The suspect allegedly followed an MIT student into the Sidney-Pacific residence hall shortly after the Harvard incident.
MIT police describe the suspect as a white male, 5'9", thin build, wearing a white t-shirt and dark shorts.

title: Police: Woman, 85, found dead in R.I. hotel after son dies by suicide
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: 85-year-old woman found dead in Rhode Island hotel is identified as Terry Costello.
Police were notifying Costello of her 66-year-old son's suicide when they discovered her body.
The death of Terry Costello is being investigated as a homicide, with no public threat identified.

title: If Bruins are looking to make a deal, the Ducks are an appealing trade partner
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Anaheim Ducks' Olen Zellweger and Mason McTavish may be viable trade targets for the Boston Bruins this offseason.
Bruins President Cam Neely emphasized the need for more speed and talent to compete with elite NHL teams.
Anaheim's financial strategies may prompt interest in trading players like Zellweger and McTavish to manage cap space.

title: Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A porbeagle shark was found stranded and later died in Point Judith Pond, Rhode Island.
The shark was initially spotted at Salty Brine State Beach before moving into shallow waters.
Porbeagles prefer colder waters; NOAA performed a necropsy after the shark died.

title: Families continue to fight BPS exam school admissions policy after judge rules no discrimination against white, Asian students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Boston Parent Coalition filed an appeal against BPS's tiered admissions, claiming it discriminates against white and Asian students.
A federal judge previously dismissed claims of discrimination, stating the tier system is consistent with prior legal precedent.
The socioeconomic-based admission system led to increased Black and Latino representation, while white and Asian percentages decreased.

title: Patriots receivers well aware of the A.J. Brown trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Rumors suggest A.J. Brown may be traded from the Eagles to the Patriots after June 1.
Patriots receiver Kyle Williams is aware of the rumors but remains focused on improving his own game.
Eagles could reduce a potential $43.3 million dead cap hit if Brown's trade occurs post-June 1.

title: Students in Maine were accidentally served dirt for dessert, school says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Three students at Medomak Valley High School accidentally ate sterilized soil served during a school event.
The soil was prepared for a science project and mistakenly identified as food during the Empty Bowl Supper.
School staff contacted the affected students' parents and confirmed the incident was accidental, not a prank.

title: What the Patriots are saying about Super Bowl rematch with Seahawks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots will face the Seattle Seahawks in a Super Bowl rematch to open the 2026 season.
Cornerback Marcus Jones expressed determination to succeed after losing 29-13 to Seattle in Super Bowl LX.
Special teams player Brenden Schooler described the upcoming game as an opportunity to showcase offseason progress.
